feed.title: Herbal-Rancher Sherri
feed.subtitle: Hey there, welcome to my site. Take a look around and let me know if you have any questions.

entry.title: Creating structure for Lab Walkthroughs!
entry.content: First zBible Study blog with GitHub and Jekyll, prep to setup content for the zBible Study. Getting the site setup and ready for content. I will be creating a folder for each book of the Bible and then posting walkthroughs for each one as I complete them. This will be a great way to document my progress and share my findings with others. Stay tuned for more updates!
entry.summary: First zBible Study blog with GitHub and Jekyll, prep to setup content for the zBible Study. Getting the site setup and ready for content. I will be creating a folder for each book of the Bible and then posting walkthroughs for each one as I complete them. This will be a great way to document my progress and share my findings with others. Stay tuned for more updates!

entry.title: Welcome to Jekyll!
entry.content:
You’ll find this post in your _posts directory. Go ahead and edit it and re-build the site to see your changes. You can rebuild the site in many different ways, but the most common way is to run jekyll serve, which launches a web server and auto-regenerates your site when a file is updated.

Jekyll requires blog post files to be named according to the following format:

YEAR-MONTH-DAY-title.MARKUP

Where YEAR is a four-digit number, MONTH and DAY are both two-digit numbers, and MARKUP is the file extension representing the format used in the file. After that, include the necessary front matter. Take a look at the source for this post to get an idea about how it works.

Jekyll also offers powerful support for code snippets:

Check out the Jekyll docs for more info on how to get the most out of Jekyll. File all bugs/feature requests at Jekyll’s GitHub repo. If you have questions, you can ask them on Jekyll Talk.
entry.summary: You’ll find this post in your _posts directory. Go ahead and edit it and re-build the site to see your changes. You can rebuild the site in many different ways, but the most common way is to run jekyll serve, which launches a web server and auto-regenerates your site when a file is updated.

entry.title: Creating structure for Lab Walkthroughs!
entry.content: First lab walkthrough blog with GitHub and Jekyll, prep to setup content for the Lab Walkthroughs. Getting the site setup and ready for content. I will be creating a folder for each lab walkthroughs! This will be a great way to document my progress and share my findings with others. Stay tuned for more updates!
entry.summary: First lab walkthrough blog with GitHub and Jekyll, prep to setup content for the Lab Walkthroughs. Getting the site setup and ready for content. I will be creating a folder for each lab walkthroughs! This will be a great way to document my progress and share my findings with others. Stay tuned for more updates!

entry.title: Welcome to Herbal Rancher Sherri’s Page!

link.title: Welcome to Herbal Rancher Sherri’s Page!

entry.content: First Calbright College and CompTIA Network+ blog with GitHub and Jekyll, prep to setup content for the Network+ assignments. Getting the site setup and ready for content. I will be creating a folder for each Network+ assignment! This will be a great way to document my progress and share my findings with others. Stay tuned for more updates!
